ability.barbarian.recruit-rage.name: Rage
ability.barbarian.recruit-rage.rulesText:
Barbarian Ability — Recruit
Action • Status • Rage

Use this character’s shared activation. Move this card from its equipped Skill Card into this character’s Status Row.

While Rage is active:
• This character’s melee weapon attacks deal +1 damage.
• This character has -1 Defense.
• This character cannot cast spells.
• This character may still use non-spell abilities, reactions, items, and normal weapon attacks.

At the end of each later shared activation belonging to this character, check whether this character personally dealt 1 or more HP damage to an enemy during that activation.
• If they did, Rage remains active.
• If they did not, return Rage to its equipped Skill Card.
• If the shared activation was entirely lost because this character was Stunned, do not check Rage. Rage remains active.

At the end of combat, return Rage to its equipped Skill Card.

source.notes: Rage uses the owner’s shared activation to begin, occupies the owner’s Status Row while active, and returns to the exact Skill Card it came from when it ends. Count 1 is the provisional core-alpha supply.

character.cleric.name: Cleric
character.cleric.rulesText:
Player Character — Divine

Maximum HP: 14
Suggested First Placement: Player Back Row

Class Trait — Sacred Relief
Once per Encounter Round, when the Cleric restores 1 or more HP to another Friendly Entity, remove 1 Heat from that entity.

Native Skill Domains
Restoration; Divine Intervention; Exorcism.

source.notes: HP, Sacred Relief, native domains, recommended starter cards, and starting defense are migrated from the starter roster. Referenced equipment and spells will be added in later bulk packs.

character.druid.name: Druid
character.druid.rulesText:
Player Character — Primal

Maximum HP: 14
Suggested First Placement: Player Back Row

Class Trait — Wild Pathfinder
While traveling on foot, whenever the Druid reveals 1 or more map tiles, reveal 1 additional adjacent face-down tile. This cannot reveal a tile beyond the map boundary.

Native Skill Domains
Biomancy; Wildcalling; Primalism.

source.notes: HP, Wild Pathfinder, native domains, recommended starter cards, and starting defense are migrated from the starter roster. Wild Pathfinder is retained even though it is outside the initial dungeon-only smoke test.

character.mage.name: Mage
character.mage.rulesText:
Player Character — Arcane

Maximum HP: 12
Suggested First Placement: Player Back Row

Class Trait — Spellweaver
The first damaging Spell the Mage casts during its own normal action in each Encounter Round deals +1 additional damage.

Native Skill Domains
Elementalism; Arcana; Necromancy.

source.notes: This replacement record updates the starter-loadout IDs to the canonical Mage starter card IDs included in this pack. Several referenced Tactical Reserve cards are expected to already exist in the catalog.

character.rogue.name: Rogue
character.rogue.rulesText:
Player Character — Martial

Maximum HP: 18
Suggested First Placement: Player Front Row

Class Trait — Shadow Step
Once per Encounter Round, immediately before or after the Rogue uses its own normal action, move the Rogue to an empty Player Front Row or Player Back Row slot. This movement does not spend an action.

Native Skill Domains
Thief; Skirmisher; Shadowcraft.

source.notes: HP, Shadow Step, native domains, starter equipment, Tactical Reserve, and baseline defense are migrated from the starter roster. The source called the initial Thief and Skirmisher ranks Novice; this catalog record intentionally uses the finalized Rogue rank name Runner.

character.warrior.name: Warrior
character.warrior.rulesText:
Player Character — Martial

Maximum HP: 20
Suggested First Placement: Player Front Row

Class Trait — Weapon Mastery
This character's melee weapon attacks ignore 1 Defense.

Native Skill Domains
Barbarian; Vanguard; Marksman.

source.notes: Maximum HP, class trait, native skill domains, starting equipment, starter skills, Tactical Reserve cards, and starting Defense are migrated from the first-playtest character roster. Several referenced catalog records have not yet been created and are intentionally retained as future references.

dungeon.goblin-warrens.goblin-alarm.name: Goblin Alarm
dungeon.goblin-warrens.goblin-alarm.rulesText:
Event

The active character gains 2 Heat.

Reveal the bottom 2 cards of the Dungeon Deck.

Add the first revealed Goblin Enemy card to an open Enemy Formation slot.

Shuffle every other revealed card back into the Dungeon Deck.

source.sourceSection: Goblin Warrens / Scrap Fortress > Goblin Alarm
source.notes: Rules text migrated from the current Goblin Warrens source. Count 2 is a provisional first-playtest density. The source text does not explicitly state the event card's own post-resolution destination, so dungeonDiscardPile is modeled as the current one-shot dungeon-event default and should be confirmed during deck-rule review.

dungeon.goblin-warrens.tripwire.name: Tripwire
dungeon.goblin-warrens.tripwire.rulesText:
Trap

A player-controlled character in the active character's current row with an equipped Rogue skill may disarm Tripwire. If so, discard Tripwire.

Otherwise, the active character takes 1 Physical damage and gains 2 Heat.

Discard Tripwire.

source.notes: Rules text migrated from the current Goblin Warrens dungeon-card source. Count 2 is a provisional first-playtest deck-density decision and can be tuned after initial dungeon runs.

enemy.goblin-archer.name: Goblin Archer
enemy.goblin-archer.rulesText:

Deal 1 physical damage, ranged.

Loot: 1 Coin.

source.notes: I created this

enemy.goblin-bombardier.name: Goblin Bombardier
enemy.goblin-bombardier.rulesText:

Deal 2 fire damage, ranged. Each other legal friendly entity takes 1 fire damage.

Loot: 3 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 1 represents one deck instance. The splash-damage rider is intentionally represented in rulesText until a confirmed structured multi-target action field is available.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 2 represents two deck instances; this file is the single shared definition. The Poison rider is intentionally represented in rulesText until a confirmed structured status-effect action field is available.

enemy.goblin-hexer.name: Goblin Hexer

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 1 represents one deck instance. The Poison rider is intentionally represented in rulesText until a confirmed structured status-effect action field is available.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 3 represents three deck instances; this file is the single shared definition.

enemy.goblin-sneak.name: Goblin Sneak
enemy.goblin-sneak.rulesText:
Backstab
Deal 2 physical damage, melee, to the legal friendly entity with the lowest current HP. Break ties by highest Heat.

Loot: 1 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 2 represents two deck instances; this file is the single shared definition. The special target-selection instruction is kept in player-facing rules text because the confirmed action schema only provides defaultHighestHeatLegalFriendlyEntity.

enemy.goblin-worg-rider.name: Goblin Worg Rider
enemy.goblin-worg-rider.rulesText:
Trampling Charge
Deal 3 physical damage, melee. If the target has at least 1 Poison status card, it gains 1 Stun status card.

Loot: 3 Coin.

source.notes: Goblin Warrens playtest enemy. Count 1 represents one deck instance. The conditional Stun rider is intentionally represented in rulesText until a confirmed structured status-effect action field is available.

enemy.goblin-worg.name: Goblin Worg

Rending Bite

source.notes: Medium body armor. Chainmail follows the established armor progression: Clothing +0, Leather +1, Scale Mail or Brigandine +2, Chainmail +3, and Full Plate +4 Defense.

hireling.roadside-sellsword.name: Roadside Sellsword
hireling.roadside-sellsword.rulesText:
Hireling — Mercenary — Autonomous Ally — Market Only

Minimum Settlement: Village
Currency Price: 14
Preferred Row: Player Front Row
HP: 6
Defense: 1

Priority:
1. Sword Cut
If this ally is in the Player Front Row, deal 2 Physical damage to the enemy in the opposing Front Row with the highest printed Attack. Gain 1 Heat.

2. Advance
If this ally is in the Player Back Row and an empty Player Front Row slot exists, move to the leftmost empty Player Front Row slot.

Defeat: Put this card into the Loot Discard Pile.

source.notes: Count 1 is intentional. This is a persistent named market card, not a disposable enemy supply. If dismissed or defeated, it enters the Loot Discard Pile and can become available again when the Loot Deck is reshuffled.

skill.arcana.novice.name: Novice Arcana
skill.arcana.novice.rulesText:
Arcana Skill — Novice

Spell Slots: 1
May Equip: Novice Arcana spells.

Passive — Arcane Foundation
No passive effect is finalized yet. This card is active for early Mage tabletop testing and should receive a finalized printed passive before production.

source.sourceSection: Mage Character Card > Starting Skills and Attached Spells
source.notes: The starter roster names Novice Arcana and Arcane Ward but the current repository does not include a separate Arcana skill-card source file. This card is intentionally playable for setup but has a clearly marked pending passive; replace Arcane Foundation when the Arcana discipline set is finalized.

skill.barbarian.recruit-feral-instinct.name: Feral Instinct
skill.barbarian.recruit-feral-instinct.rulesText:
Barbarian Skill — Recruit

Ability Slots: 2
May Equip: Recruit Barbarian abilities.

Passive — Rising Fury
The first time this character deals 1 or more HP damage to an enemy during each of their shared activations, gain 1 additional Heat.

source.sourceSection: Barbarian Skill Cards > Recruit: Feral Instinct
source.notes: This is the rank-one Barbarian Skill Card. Its two slots can only hold Recruit-rank Barbarian Ability Cards. Count 1 is the provisional first-playtest supply.

skill.elementalism.novice.name: Novice Elementalism
skill.elementalism.novice.rulesText:
Elementalism Skill — Novice

Spell Slots: 1
May Equip: Novice Elementalism spells.

Passive — Attuned Casting
Whenever one of your Fire, Ice, Lightning, or Earth spells deals damage to a target, the first 1 damage dealt to that target by that spell ignores Defense.

source.notes: Spell-slot count and Attuned Casting are migrated from the Elementalism card file.

spell.arcana.novice-arcane-ward.name: Arcane Ward
spell.arcana.novice-arcane-ward.rulesText:
Arcana Spell — Novice
Action • Persistent Positive Effect

Use this character's shared activation. Move this card from its equipped Skill Card into the Mage's Status Row.

The Mage gains +1 Defense until the start of the Mage's next shared activation.

When this effect ends, return this card to Novice Arcana.

source.sourceSection: Mage Required Starter Companion Card Text > Arcane Ward
source.notes: Text and duration are migrated from the Mage starter roster. The generic Status Row UI can move this card to the Mage's Status Row because activation.moveTo is ownerStatusRow.

spell.elementalism.novice-firebolt.name: Firebolt
spell.elementalism.novice-firebolt.rulesText:
Elementalism Spell — Novice
Action • Spell Attack

Deal 2 Fire damage to 1 enemy in either opposing row.
Gain 1 Heat.

source.sourceSection: Mage Required Starter Companion Card Text; Novice Elementalism Spells
source.notes: The Mage starter roster specifies 2 Fire damage and gain 1 Heat. The older Elementalism file defines Fire Bolt as 1 Fire damage with no Heat. This pack intentionally follows the newer starter-roster version for first-playtest Mage setup; reconcile the source conflict before print finalization.

status.negative.poisoned.name: Poisoned
status.negative.poisoned.rulesText:
Negative Status — Poison

At the start of the affected entity's next applicable activation, that entity takes 1 Poison damage. Then discard this card.

Poisoned stacks. Resolve each Poisoned card separately.

source.notes: Count 8 is a provisional first-playtest Status Deck supply. Each Poisoned card is a separate stackable status card and resolves independently.

status.negative.stunned.name: Stunned
status.negative.stunned.rulesText:
Negative Status — Stun

At the start of the affected entity's next applicable activation, that entity loses its next normal activation. Then discard this card.

Player Character: That character's group loses its shared activation and cannot activate a Controlled Ally.
Controlled Ally: This ally cannot be selected for its owner's shared activation. The owner may still act or select another eligible Controlled Ally.
Autonomous Ally: This ally loses only its independent activation.

Stun does not prevent passive effects, statuses, reactions, or triggered abilities unless they require the Stunned entity to take an action.

exception.rule: An effect remains allowed only when it does not require the Stunned entity to take an action.

source.sourceSection: 7.9.1 Negative Status Effects; 7.9.3 Status Timing; 7.9.4 Stun and Activations
source.notes: Count 4 is a provisional first-playtest Status Deck supply. Stunned does not stack. It is intentionally modeled by affected entity type because player characters, Controlled Allies, Autonomous Allies, enemies, and bosses have different activation structures.

tactical-reserve.antidote.name: Antidote
tactical-reserve.antidote.rulesText:
Tactical Reserve — Action

Remove 1 Poisoned status card from 1 Friendly Entity.

After resolving, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.sourceSection: Shared Starting Tactical Reserve Cards > Antidote
source.notes: Count 8 covers the five starter-character references and leaves three additional copies for early Loot Deck use.

tactical-reserve.bandage.name: Bandage
tactical-reserve.bandage.rulesText:
Tactical Reserve — Action

Remove Bleeding from 1 Friendly Entity.
If that entity has no Bleeding card, restore 1 HP instead.

After resolving, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.sourceSection: Shared Starting Tactical Reserve Cards > Bandage
source.notes: Count 4 is a provisional module supply. The Bleeding status card has not yet been converted to the catalog.

tactical-reserve.barkskin-salve.name: Barkskin Salve
tactical-reserve.barkskin-salve.rulesText:
Tactical Reserve — Action — Persistent Positive Effect

Place this card in 1 Friendly Entity's Status Row. That entity gains +1 Defense until the start of its next activation.

When this effect ends, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.sourceSection: Shared Starting Tactical Reserve Cards > Barkskin Salve
source.notes: Count 4 is a provisional module supply. This is a Tactical Reserve card that occupies the target's Status Row while active.

tactical-reserve.healing-potion.name: Healing Potion
tactical-reserve.healing-potion.rulesText: Restore 3 HP to one friendly entity. After resolving, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.notes: Bootstrap sample only. Confirm the final Healing Potion wording and healing amount against the approved Tactical Reserve card design before treating this record as print-ready.

tactical-reserve.holy-water.name: Holy Water
tactical-reserve.holy-water.rulesText:
Tactical Reserve — Action

Deal 2 Force damage to 1 enemy in either opposing row. If that enemy has the Undead tag, deal +2 damage instead.

After resolving, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.sourceSection: Shared Starting Tactical Reserve Cards > Holy Water
source.notes: Count 4 is a provisional module supply.

tactical-reserve.scroll-of-force-dart.name: Scroll of Force Dart
tactical-reserve.scroll-of-force-dart.rulesText:
Tactical Reserve — Action

Deal 2 Force damage to 1 enemy in either opposing row. Gain 1 Heat.

After resolving, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.sourceSection: Shared Starting Tactical Reserve Cards > Scroll of Force Dart
source.notes: Count 4 is a provisional module supply.

tactical-reserve.smoke-bomb.name: Smoke Bomb
tactical-reserve.smoke-bomb.rulesText:
Tactical Reserve — Reaction

Use when the Rogue is chosen as the target of an enemy attack.
Prevent 2 damage from that attack. Then move the Rogue to an empty Player Back Row slot, if able.

After resolving, shuffle this card into the Loot Deck.

source.sourceSection: Shared Starting Tactical Reserve Cards > Smoke Bomb
source.notes: Count 4 is a provisional module supply. The printed trigger is Rogue-specific.

deck.goblin-warrens-catalog-smoke-test.name: Goblin Warrens Catalog Coverage Test
deck.goblin-warrens-catalog-smoke-test.rulesText: A Goblin Warrens dungeon deck for catalog coverage and early manual playtesting. This definition is the source of truth for all base Dungeon Deck cards. The selected scenario may separately inject Loot Deck cards during fresh-state setup.

knownLimitations: This deck is intended for catalog coverage and early manual playtesting, not final encounter balance.
This deck has no dedicated boss, objective, room, reward, or completion-condition cards yet.
Goblin Alarm can reveal cards from the bottom of the Dungeon Deck and deploy the first matching Goblin enemy it finds.
Scenario-level Loot Deck injection is deliberately not listed here because it belongs to the selected scenario.

source.notes: Generated from all active enemy.goblin-* definitions plus active Goblin Warrens Tripwire and Goblin Alarm definitions. Base deck total: 32.

scenario.solo-warrior-goblin-warrens-smo.name: Solo Warrior: Goblin Warrens Smoke Test
scenario.solo-warrior-goblin-warrens-smo.rulesText: A minimal manual playtest setup for validating scenario loading, card-instance creation, equipment, Skill Card attachments, Tactical Reserve placement, Dungeon Deck shuffling, manual drawing, enemy deployment, and Dungeon Discard behavior.

endCondition.notes: This is a catalog and tabletop smoke test, not a complete dungeon. Stop when the intended card, deck, row, counter, and discard interactions have been tested.

knownLimitations: This scenario does not yet include a full starting loadout.
This scenario does not yet include a boss, dungeon objective, or normal dungeon-completion condition.
This scenario intentionally begins with only one filled Tactical Reserve slot.
Combat, targeting, damage, Heat, and status resolution remain manually controlled during this early playtest stage.

source.notes: First scenario-definition test. It references only currently cataloged active card and deck definitions, and intentionally avoids the Warrior card's unconverted starter-loadout references.
